GrowthX is we're looking at both sides of search, right?
Traditional isn't going away, but AI search, there's a massive opportunity right now.
And if you're not in the answer, prospects sort of see it as like you don't exist, which is less of a problem for ZoomInfo with the brand recognition that you have.

But there's always an opportunity, right, to show up in more places if you can embrace creating for the long tail, having that surface area of content that can address all the different ways that someone would go in and search.

And so what we've seen, and I'm sure you've seen it as well, is if you can show up in Answers, you can get higher intent traffic, 4.4 times more likely to convert, spending three times as long on your site, really just, you know, coming in with a lot more education.
And so that's our focus, right, is to basically have these workflows that go through the most important factors for AEO or GEO, be able to customize them from client to client and do all this work.
Work around the context engineering to really like deliver content that is at a much higher quality and much more personal to your brand than what you might be able to build internally.

Any questions at a high level?

Exec, we've done a lot more of the sales content for them.
They're more like coaching and training.

In the first phase, we'll do an eight-week strategy sprint.

You know, publishing every business day of the week.
And so this is sort of how we split up the partnership.

Like, who's doing what in this scenario?
Yeah, it's mainly our team that's doing the work.

And we collaborate with you in the strategy sprint by ingesting resources ahead of the kickoff call and then having a kickoff call where we're going to dig into some specific pieces of, you know, your business, your ICP, your products and services.
And then we'll move into like a product deep dive.
And so once we've done that, you know, a good example to show you, Molly, would be Engine.

But haters...

And so, this is, like, a little bit of, you know, what we're doing in terms of the context engineering.

And optimize the next time we create a piece of content.

I think there's just always a question.
Like, I've worked with a lot of vendors in past, like, the SEO days, less the, you know, AI search days, that are, like, content vendors.
And I think there's always this struggle when you talk about workflow and, like, how the teams work together of, like, getting the teams really to function as one.

And so I'm just curious, like,

Like how you guys manage that partnership and like manage the workflows where it really feels like it's seamless across teams and it's not like these cold vendor handoffs and just like talk me through how that works.
Yeah, I think the key is that we have like one, two, or three at the most points of review.
If it's a situation where like there's a team of reviewers or it has to go to multiple teams, we just hit this bottleneck around quantity that we need to get to and volume.
I would say as well, like defining, you know, what is GrowthX working on and what is the internal team working on is really important.
And at the end of the day, there's got to be a need, right, for us to actually come in and create content on your behalf.
If there isn't much of a need, then, you know, I agree there's like friction between the two teams.
Cool.

please.

interesting.
